title: BCIT Advanced Guard Duty
description: The Advanced Guard Duty dataset contains EEG, eye tracking, and behavioral data collected during a sustained vigilance task involving verification of replica identification cards. Subjects performed a 50-minute guard duty task divided into ten 5-minute blocks with varying ID presentation rates (1-25 per minute) and verification criteria. The dataset includes 256-channel EEG recordings, eye tracking data, driving simulator vehicle dynamics, and behavioral responses, designed to investigate cognitive and physiological correlates of sustained attention and fatigue in realistic operational settings.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using BioSemi 256-channel systems (plus 8 additional channels including 4 eye and 2 mastoid channels) at 1024 Hz sampling rate. Eye tracking was performed using Sensomotoric Instruments REDEYE250. Subjects performed a guard duty task involving serial presentation of replica ID cards paired with reference face images from the Multi-PIE database. The task consisted of ten 5-minute blocks with ID presentation rates varying stochastically between 1 and 25 per minute according to predefined profiles. Participants made forced-choice allow/deny decisions with maximum response time of 20 seconds per trial.
